conditions.today: 6 AM—Up and at 'em, Jackson. The strangely engaging season of 2026 rolls on. After yesterday's warm and windy weather, we're in for a Thursday that'll feel more "cool and calm" across the land. And, with a one-inch touchup overnight, good times galore will seem to pool in the palm of your hand. Sunflakes and snowshine will reverberate off the refurbished terrain as a few passing clouds drop long shadows on the slopes. Cooler high temperatures will top out near 20 at the summit and in the mid-to upper 30s down in the village. The winds have diminished but will still be gusting out of the west between 10 and 25 mph, especially in the upper elevations. Slashing secret stashes can lead to a rapid chain reaction of euphoric synaptic flashes. Because pow blasting is a passion that nothing surpasses, a vibe more valuable than cash is. -Lege
conditions.yesterday: 6 AM—This is not a test. The Emergency Pow-Cast System has been activated following 24 hours of wild weather that has slathered the mountainside with 14 inches of fresh snow...so far. All vertically inclined citizens are being asked to gather their burliest storm-skiing gear and get out here, as wind-driven powder and low-elevation liquid are expected to blast the Tetons through the day. Mild high temperatures will tickle the 30-degree mark at the summit and approach 40 degrees down in the valley. Instead of cheeky rhyming, today I'm going to do a little reminding. Pack some extra patience in your pack, as ski patrol will be working hard to get you safely into the goods as quickly as possible. Heavy accumulations and these strong west-southwest winds are obviously a blessing, but they can also be a bit of a curse, as delays and lift closures may be necessary. Just enjoy the elevated atmospheric energy and be ready for an alpine experience that's extraordinary. -Lege
